text: Carbon dioxide (CO2) activation and subsequent hydrogenation to methanol is a crucial reaction for utilizing the captured CO2. In the present study, the CO2 reduction catalytic performance using size-selected Cu4 clusters supported on Al2O3 thin films has been investigated in the presence of hydrogen. The experiments were carried out under the near-atmospheric reaction conditions with a low CO2 partial pressure. The oxidation state of the Cu clusters was identified by means of in situ grazing incidence X-ray absorption spectroscopy. The size-selected Cu4 clusters on the Al2O3 thin films support showed the highest catalytic activity towards the low pressure catalytic conversion of CO2 to CH3OH. The density functional theory (DFT) calculation revealed that the Cu4 clusters had low activation energies for the conversion of CO2 to CH3OH. Thus the small Cu clusters might be potential candidates for efficient catalysts in recycling the released CO2.
